sources_audited.note: The Census manual supports the exact 336991 scope and stated exclusions, but it does not measure the eligible or ownable fractions for e or o; those remain explicitly labeled estimates.

BLS supports the stated broad NAICS 336000 occupational mix, including 52.71% production occupations and 7.25% business and financial operations, but does not observe a task-exposure share for 336991.
NIST supports the cited manufacturing AI use cases and the cost, skills, data, cybersecurity, and legacy-system barriers, but does not quantify a or rho for this six-digit industry.
Gallup reports that 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership in the next five years, matching the stated cross-industry succession proxy.

The 2025 Harley-Davidson 10-K supports the manufacturer revenue model, independent-dealer channel, parts and accessories activity, and 12.4% retail-unit contraction, but one large premium public company cannot establish LMM-wide e, q, d5, or o.

PeopleForBikes supports bicycle sales softness, pandemic pull-forward, discounting, price increases, and relative strength in service, helmets, and maintenance tools, but covers only the bicycle portion of the industry and does not directly quantify q or d5.

PeopleForBikes supports record 2024 participation and increased youth participation, but participation is only directional evidence for purchases and covers neither motorcycles nor the full parts mix.

caveats: The numerical a and rho ranges are reasoned cross-manufacturing proxies rather than measurements for 336991; the packet discloses this and the low case already captures a tier-relevant downside.
The 22% five-year succession input is an all-industry intention measure, not an observed rate of completed control sales by niche manufacturers.
S1 establishes industry scope but does not independently support the estimated e or o fractions, which depend on unverified candidate-level ownership and transferability judgments.
Harley-Davidson is a large premium public manufacturer and cannot represent the economics, ownership availability, or demand of lower-middle-market component and niche-vehicle firms.
The PeopleForBikes evidence covers bicycles only, and increased participation does not directly establish new-product demand or value capture across motorcycles and components.

The packet marks the lens as non-heterogeneous while its own narrative and ranges describe materially different motorcycle, bicycle, and parts subsegments; this inconsistency does not change the base tier or MODEL_CONDITIONED action because the uncertainty is otherwise disclosed.
summary: Mechanics pass and all seven primitive-cited sources were reopened. The record is useful and generally candid: broad sources are labeled as proxies, unsupported exact fractions are labeled as estimates, ranges expose downside to STRUCTURAL_OUT, and the narrative follows the evidence. The main weaknesses are limited representativeness, indirect support for several numeric inputs, and a non-material inconsistency in the heterogeneity flag, so the appropriate disposition is publishable_with_caveats rather than reject.
